introSection: Canobie Corkscrew was a steel roller coaster located at Canobie Lake Park in Salem, New Hampshire, USA. It opened at its current location in 1987.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster first opened in 1975 as Chicago Loop with the indoor shopping center and amusement park Old Chicago in Illinois. The coaster operated for five years until 1980. The coaster then moved to Alabama State Fairgrounds in Birmingham, Alabama, USA. It operated in Alabama from 1982 to 1986.

For the 1987 season, the coaster was relocated to Canobie Lake Park in New Hampshire.

For over two decades, Canobie Corkscrew was the only ride in the park to invert riders until Untamed, a Gerstlauer Euro-Fighter, opened in 2011.

In 2012, Canobie Corkscrew underwent a repainting in which the track was painted sky blue and the supports painted silver. The red trains were painted blue.

In 2021, Canobie Lake Park announced that the closure of Canobie Corkscrew would be permanent. The track and supports from the second corkscrew were donated to the National Roller Coaster Museum. It took five flat bed trucks to move the cars, track, and supports from New Hampshire to Texas.

The ride's corkscrew was reassembled at the National Roller Coaster Museum in late 2025. It will serve as an entry marquee for cars that drive into the parking lot.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Riders made a right out of the station and began ascending the chain lift hill. A banked turn lead to the right, and then a single drop, followed by a 180 degree banked turn, which lead into the double corkscrew element and then a turn back into the station.

sections.title: Canobie Lake Park
sections.content: A sky blue track and silver supports, used to be a yellow track and black supports.

sections.title: Old Chicago & Alabama State Fairgrounds
sections.content: A white track and supports, the coaster also used this color scheme when at Alabama State Fairgrounds.

fullText:
Canobie Corkscrew was a steel roller coaster located at Canobie Lake Park in Salem, New Hampshire, USA. It opened at its current location in 1987.

The roller coaster first opened in 1975 as Chicago Loop with the indoor shopping center and amusement park Old Chicago in Illinois. The coaster operated for five years until 1980. The coaster then moved to Alabama State Fairgrounds in Birmingham, Alabama, USA. It operated in Alabama from 1982 to 1986.

For the 1987 season, the coaster was relocated to Canobie Lake Park in New Hampshire.

For over two decades, Canobie Corkscrew was the only ride in the park to invert riders until Untamed, a Gerstlauer Euro-Fighter, opened in 2011.

In 2012, Canobie Corkscrew underwent a repainting in which the track was painted sky blue and the supports painted silver. The red trains were painted blue.

In 2021, Canobie Lake Park announced that the closure of Canobie Corkscrew would be permanent. The track and supports from the second corkscrew were donated to the National Roller Coaster Museum. It took five flat bed trucks to move the cars, track, and supports from New Hampshire to Texas.

The ride's corkscrew was reassembled at the National Roller Coaster Museum in late 2025. It will serve as an entry marquee for cars that drive into the parking lot.

Riders made a right out of the station and began ascending the chain lift hill. A banked turn lead to the right, and then a single drop, followed by a 180 degree banked turn, which lead into the double corkscrew element and then a turn back into the station.



A sky blue track and silver supports, used to be a yellow track and black supports.

A white track and supports, the coaster also used this color scheme when at Alabama State Fairgrounds.
